task_completion.description: The Jupyter Notebook bid_rent_analysis.ipynb is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jupyter Notebook interface is visible with the file 'bid_rent_analysis.ipynb' open.

task_completion.description: The Central Business District (CBD) is identified and its coordinates are established.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells are visible that link jobs, buildings, and parcels to find the zone with the highest jobs (should be Zone 2.0) and calculate the mean x and y coordinates of its parcels.

task_completion.description: Euclidean distance to the CBD and value intensity are calculated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells show the calculation of Euclidean distance from each parcel to the CBD coordinates, and the calculation of value intensity as residential sales price divided by shape area.

task_completion.description: The Bid-Rent Curve is calculated and saved to a CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells show grouping parcels into 1,000-unit distance bins, calculating the median value per sqft, and saving the dataframe to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/bid_rent_curve.csv'.

task_completion.description: The top 50 remote high-value anomalies are identified and saved to a CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells show filtering parcels with distance > 10,000, selecting the top 50 by value intensity, and saving the dataframe to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/value_anomalies.csv'.

task_completion.description: A visualization of the distance bins versus median value is generated and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plot is visible in the notebook showing distance bins on the x-axis and median value on the y-axis, along with code saving it to '/home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/bid_rent_chart.png'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is located at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. The Central Business District (CBD) should be identified as Zone 2.0 with coordinates approximately x=552648.5, y=4182938.5. The bid_rent_curve.csv should contain median values for 1,000-unit distance bins. The value_anomalies.csv must contain exactly 50 parcels with a distance strictly greater than 10,000 units, and the top anomaly has an intensity value of ~11.08/sqft (parcel ID 63277).
